data.title: Meta looking to enter power-trading business as AI demand surges
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) is looking to enter the wholesale power-trading business so it can better manage the electricity needs of its data centers.

Meta filed the application to the U.S. Federal Energy Regulatory Commission this week seeking authorization for the move. The subsidiary "has been formed to act as a power marketer to sell energy,capacity, and certain ancillary services at wholesale in the United States," according to the filing.

The company has requested the regulator to approve its application by Nov. 16.

Meta did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

A Meta representative told Bloomberg News that it was a natural next step to participate in energy markets as it looks to power its operations with clean energy.

The company would be required to file for membership so that it can trade in one of seven competitive power markets, such as the Texas grid or the Midcontinent Independent System Operator, that encompasses the Louisiana data center, the report added.

Power demand from data centers used to build and run AI models is set to quadruple in 10 years, based on estimates from BloombergNEF, the report noted. Simultaneously, power prices are rising amid this surge in electric demand.

Acquiring electricity has become an urgent need for technology companies, including Meta, Microsoft (MSFT) and Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Google. A massive amount of electricity is needed to run data centers, which also helps run AI technology.

data.title: Elon Musk's XAI raises $10B at $200B valuation - CNBC
data.description:
• Elon Musk's XAI raised $10 billion at a $200 billion valuation, according to a CNBC report.
  • XAI is raising the funds from a number of investors, and that may include Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund, according to CNBC's David Faber, who cited people familiar with the matter.

data.title: DexCom extends drop to reach five-month low
data.description:
Shares of DexCom (NASDAQ:DXCM) fell for the third straight session on Friday to reach the lowest level since April, even as the continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) equipment maker managed to retain its bullish views on Wall Street despite a short report on Thursday.

TD Cowen and Baird were among the firms to reiterate their buy-equivalent ratings after Hunterbrook Capital issued a short report on the stock, for which the maker of the G7 CGM System has yet to respond to Seeking Alpha’s requests for comments.

TD Cowen’s Joshua Jennings, who maintained a Buy rating and $100 per share target, noted that the short report validates the bear thesis on the G7 sensor's reliability, and a “solid” Q3 report could be vital to quash concerns.

“Much of this concern is already embedded in valuation, and our endocrinologist checks plus prescription trends indicate DXCM is not losing share at present,” Bloomberg News reported, quoting Jennings.

While there is a risk that reliability issues can escalate and hurt demand, “management asserts they’re managing a minor issue and remain confident in achieving 2025 targets.”

However, Baird analyst Jeff Johnson wrote that there is a noteworthy increase in G6/G7 "injury" reports for July/August, which, according to him, could indicate that DXCM's earlier comments regarding stable G7 warranty claims/other issues could no longer be valid.

“We’re reserving judgment about potential 2025-2026 financial implications for DXCM, but we’re incrementally cautious on this name pending [Seagrove] survey results,” Johnson wrote, referring to market data on the CGM market. He maintained his Outperform rating and a $112 target on DXCM.

data.title: The new ESPN DTC service and Fox One together get 1M sign-ups in 10 days
data.description:
The new ESPN direct-to-consumer service (NYSE:DIS) and Fox One (NASDAQ:FOX) (NASDAQ:FOXA) have managed to get one million sign-ups cumulatively in a span of 10 days, according to a report by Antenna, a research firm that closely follows the streaming industry.

The Antenna report didn’t clearly indicate how many people signed up for ESPN and Fox One. But according to Front Office Sports, most of the subscribers are for ESPN.

The research firm observed that 4 in 5 sign-ups for ESPN Ultimate plan between August 21 and 31 came through a bundle, and almost 60% of all sign-ups for FOX One between August 21 and 31 came via an Amazon-distributed channel.

The firm also estimated that more than 10% of sign-ups to the new ESPN service chose an annual plan.

data.title: AMC Entertainment reportedly collaborating with Taylor Swift for her 'secret' theatrical event
data.description:
According to a Friday report by The Hollywood Reporter, pop music artist Taylor Swift is planning a "secret" theatrical event that will be tied to her upcoming album "The Life of a Showgirl" in collaboration with U.S. theater operator AMC Entertainment (NYSE:AMC). 

AMC shares were up over 7% after midday, while shares of other U.S. theater companies had a muted reaction to the report.

The report also said that Swift and her team financed the $15M project in which AMC is involved and bypassed the traditional Hollywood studio system.

A Variety report suggested that the secret theatrical event would be a release party film of her new album and will run for 89 minutes. 

The report said "Taylor Swift: The Official Release Party of a Showgirl" will be shown in all 540 AMC locations in the U.S. starting October 3, which is also the release date for her new album.

data.title: E-commerce accelerator Pattern Group begins trading slightly below the IPO pricing level
data.description:
Pattern Group (NASDAQ:PTRN) opened for trading early Friday afternoon at $13.50 before settling back to $13.39 at 1:10 p.m. The IPO was priced at $14.00, which was the middle of the anticipated range. Co-founder and CEO Mel Alder rang the opening bell on the Nasdaq earlier in the day.

Pattern Group was originally founded as iServe in 2013 in Lehi, Utah, by David Wright and Melanie Alder. By 2018, the company rebranded from iServe to Pattern and expanded internationally with the acquisition of the consultancy Practicology.

The Utah-based company uses advanced AI and machine learning to manage logistics, pricing, advertising, and customer service for its partners. Its specialty is managing branded sales on digital marketplaces like Amazon (AMZN), Walmart (WMT), Target (TGT), and TikTok Shop

Pattern Group (NASDAQ:PTRN) disclosed that it generated revenue of $1.8 billion for the year that ended on December 31, 2024, which represents a CAGR of 35% over the last two years. During the same period, the company's net income improved from -$3 million to $68 million. Adjusted EBITDA during 2024 was $101 million, which represented a CAGR of 138% over the prior two years.

Notably, Pattern Group (PTRN) derived 94% of its revenue from consumer product sales on Amazon (AMZN). In addition, Amazon (AMZN) accounted for over 75% of the total U.S. marketplace retail sales in 2024. For the first six months of 2025, Pattern Group (PTRN) reported a 35% year-over-year jump in revenue.

E-commerce players that Pattern Group (PTRN) competes with include MarketLeap, Jodaro, Assiduus Global, Spreetail, Quince, Dianxiaomi, CloudCommerce, Trend, Shopify (SHOP), and Texture.

data.title: Capstone Copper resumes full operations at Mantoverde mine
data.description:
Capstone Copper (OTCPK:CSCCF) said late Thursday it resumed full sulphide copper production at its Mantoverde mine in Chile, following repairs to a ball mill motor that had interrupted production since late August.

Capstone (OTCPK:CSCCF) reported on September 1 that a second motor failure had curbed output at Mantoverde, reducing production by 3,000-4,000 tons of copper concentrate while repairs were completed; the mine was forced to operate by bypassing one of its two ball mills after the breakdown on August 30.

The company said at the time it would mitigate the impact by advancing planned maintenance, while also preparing a long-term replacement and repair strategy.

Five days of planned maintenance are still expected to be conducted at the end of September, the company said.

data.title: Judge dismisses Trump's $15B NYT lawsuit for violating court rules
data.description:
A Florida judge on Friday temporarily dismissed U.S. President Donald Trump's $15B lawsuit against The New York Times (NYSE:NYT) for being too long and violating court rules.

According to U.S. District Judge Steven Merryday, Trump's lawyers did not follow the court rules that require "short, plain, direct" statements outlining why the plaintiff deserves relief.

However, the judge said that his lawyers can file an amended suit by next month.

The judge claimed that the Trump lawsuit "unmistakably and inexcusably" violated federal court rules by presenting allegations that were "repetitive," "superfluous," and "florid." 

Judge Merriday also said that his complaint is not “a public forum for vituperation and invective” or “a protected platform to rage against an adversary.”

Trump had launched the lawsuit against the newspaper earlier this week and accused it of being a mouthpiece for the Democratic Party and for having an agenda against him.

He accused the paper of having "engaged in decades long method of lying" and pointed to concerns such as the Times’ endorsement of former presidential candidate Kamala Harris.

NYT shares were up marginally and did not have a significant reaction to the news.

The lawsuit is President Donald J. Trump v. New York Times Company, case no. 8:25-cv-02487, filed in the United States District Court, Middle District of Florida, Tampa Division.  

More on New York Times

data.title: Lowe's Companies to acquire ASP Flag Parent Holdings for ~$8.8B
data.description:
Lowe's Companies (NYSE:LOW) said in an SEC filing on Friday that it will acquire all the shares of Delaware corporation, ASP Flag Parent Holdings, for ~$8.8B.

The home improvement company has entered into a credit agreement for a $2B unsecured revolving loan facility to replace its existing $2B unsecured revolving credit facility.

Also, the company has signed a term loan credit agreement for a $2B unsecured term loan facility. Additionally, Lowe's said an aggregate amount of up to $5B in bridge facility commitments remain.
